product_overview: termcn is a public marketing and documentation site for a registry of terminal UI components (React with Ink and OpenTUI) delivered in a shadcn-style workflow. The site provides a landing experience, MDX-based docs with navigation aids, and text exports for LLM/tooling consumption.
core_goals: Help visitors understand what termcn is and how to install components.
Allow developers to browse MDX documentation, examples, and registry metadata without signing in.
Ensure site navigation (header, sidebar, prev/next) surfaces documentation and CTAs reliably; the footer intentionally shows credits/links only (no docs links).

features.name: Browse marketing home
features.description: Public landing page that introduces termcn, shows component preview demos, and links users into the documentation.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Scroll hero and marketing sections -> View embedded component preview cards on the homepage -> Click primary navigation links (e.g. Get Started / Docs) -> Navigate to /docs
Navigate to / -> View embedded component preview cards on the homepage -> Scroll hero and marketing sections

features.name: Read documentation
features.description: MDX-driven documentation shell with sidebar navigation, table of contents anchors for long pages, copy-page control, and previous/next article navigation.
user_flows: Navigate to /docs -> Navigate to /docs/components -> Scroll long pages and use table of contents anchors -> Use previous/next article navigation between pages
Navigate to / -> Click primary navigation link to open /docs -> Navigate to /docs/installation -> Use copy-page or external doc links where offered
Navigate to /docs -> Navigate sidebar to a doc page -> Scroll long pages and use table of contents anchors -> Use copy-page or external doc links where offered

features.name: LLM-oriented exports
features.description: Serve plain-text and full-text exports intended for tooling and LLM context extraction, accessible without authentication.
user_flows: Navigate to /llms.txt -> Observe plain-text export content
Navigate to /llms-full.txt -> Observe full export content

tech_stack: Ink and OpenTUI (documented terminal UI libraries)

routes.description: Marketing home with hero, CTAs, and embedded component preview demos
Documentation index and nested doc pages (installation, components, theming, registry, MCP, templates, etc.); changelog may exist but is not linked in navigation.
Per-page raw markdown export for each docs slug (processed MDX as markdown)

features.name: Browse marketing home
features.description: Landing page explains termcn (terminal UIs for React with Ink and OpenTUI) and links into documentation
user_interactions: Click primary navigation links (e.g. Get Started / Docs)
View embedded component preview cards on the homepage

features.name: Read documentation
features.description: MDX-based docs with sidebar, breadcrumbs, table of contents, copy-page control, and prev/next navigation
user_interactions: Open docs from header or home CTA
Navigate sidebar to a doc page
Scroll long pages and use table of contents anchors
Use copy-page or external doc links where offered
Use previous/next article navigation between pages

features.name: LLM-oriented exports
features.description: Serves aggregated text/markdown exports for tooling and LLM context
user_interactions: Request /llms.txt or /llms-full.txt in browser or client

known_limitations.issue: Very large number of static doc URLs; exhaustive UI enumeration is impractical in one pass
known_limitations.impact: Automated tests should focus on representative docs paths and global navigation shell
